title: What is MCP? Learn MCP Client, MCP Server & MCP Architecture
summary: Model Context Protocol (MCP) is a standard that enables AI agents and applications to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a unified interface. The protocol uses JSON-RPC for client-server communication and supports multiple transport mechanisms including STDIO and HTTP.
writeup: This video explains the Model Context Protocol (MCP), a standard that lets AI agents and applications connect to external tools, APIs, databases, and services through a unified interface. It breaks down the MCP architecture into client and server roles, covering how the two communicate using JSON-RPC. Key takeaways: MCP standardizes API integration for AI agents, and it supports multiple transport mechanisms including STDIO and HTTP.
